generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance LiDARPointCloudSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-resolution LiDAR sensor capturing raw 2D/3D point clouds for object detection and tracking in the autonomous vehicle environment."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudDataTransmitted
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor suite capturing vehicle state data including speed, position, orientation, and motor actuation signals for ACC system monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateDataTransmitted
]

instance MotorActuationSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensors monitoring motor actuation signals such as torque, RPM, and voltage commands for real-time vehicle control feedback."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorActuationDataTransmitted
]

instance SteeringServoSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensors capturing steering servo actuation signals including wheel angle commands for adaptive path control."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SteeringServoDataTransmitted
]

instance ObjectTrackingSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Processed sensor data providing point cloud clustering and tracking results for detected objects (e.g., lead vehicles, obstacles)."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ObjectTrackingDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance LiDARPointCloudDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Raw LiDAR point cloud data transmitted from the LiDARPointCloudSensor in real-time for object detection processing."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARPointCloudSensor
]

instance VehicleStateDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Vehicle state data including speed, position, and orientation transmitted for real-time monitoring and ACC computations."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

instance MotorActuationDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Motor actuation signals (torque, RPM, voltage) transmitted for real-time control feedback and system validation."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorActuationSensor
]

instance SteeringServoDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Steering servo actuation signals (wheel angle commands) transmitted for adaptive path control and trajectory validation."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringServoSensor
]

instance ObjectTrackingDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Processed object tracking data including clustered point clouds and validated trajectories for lead vehicle and obstacle detection."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ObjectTrackingSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance PointCloudClusteringModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Software component converting raw LiDAR point clouds into structured object tracks and trajectories, reducing dimensionality and noise."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudProcessingEnabler
]

instance DataTranslationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Functional entity serializing data, aligning message types, and performing unit conversions (e.g., SI to engineering units) for all data streams."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured vehicle state data including speed, position, and orientation for real-time monitoring and ACC computations."
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreProcessingEnabler, VisualizationEnabler
]

instance MotorActuationData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed motor actuation signals (torque, RPM, voltage) for real-time control feedback and system validation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorActuationDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreProcessingEnabler, ActuationValidationEnabler
]

instance SteeringServoData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed steering servo actuation signals (wheel angle commands) for adaptive path control and trajectory validation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SteeringServoDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreProcessingEnabler, PathPlanningEnabler
]

instance ObjectTrackingData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed object tracking data including clustered point clouds and validated trajectories for lead vehicle and obstacle detection."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ObjectTrackingDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreProcessingEnabler, VisualizationEnabler, ACCComputationEnabler
]

instance RawLiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw LiDAR point cloud data transmitted for object detection processing before clustering and tracking."
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudProcessingEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance PointCloudProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Software component implementing point cloud clustering and tracking algorithms to convert raw LiDAR data into structured object tracks."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance DataTranslationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Functional entity responsible for serializing data, aligning message types, and performing unit conversions for all transmitted data streams."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance DataPreProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Software component filtering and normalizing sensor inputs (LiDAR, motor, steering) before transmission to ensure data quality."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance VisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "3D visualization component rendering processed data (object tracks, driving path) for real-time monitoring in the user interface."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance ACCComputationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Cloud-based computation module handling lead vehicle detection, desired speed computation, and actuation command generation for ACC."
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance ActuationValidationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Component validating and ensuring safety of actuation commands before execution by on-board actuators."
    DTDFVocab:enables RemoteManagementService
]

instance PathPlanningEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Component generating and validating driving paths based on object tracking and vehicle state data."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance ROS2MiddlewareEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Robot Operating System 2 (ROS2) middleware aggregating heterogeneous data streams (LiDAR, vehicle state, actuation) into a unified topic-based architecture."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance SQLDatabaseEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SQL database storing structured vehicle state data, object tracks, and ACC logs for historical analysis and reporting."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance MATLABSimulinkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MATLAB/Simulink environment for modeling complex ACC algorithms (control logic, path planning) and co-simulation with ROS2."
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance WebDashboardEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Web-based dashboard implemented with JavaScript/HTML/CSS for cross-platform accessibility and real-time data streaming via ROS2."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, RemoteManagementService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization, logging, and reporting of vehicle state data for operators and developers, including projected driving paths and detected object tracks."
    DTDFVocab:provides VehicleStateInsight, DrivingPathInsight, ObjectTrackInsight, ReportGenerationAction
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Cloud-based computation delegation offloading the entire ACC system from the vehicle, including lead vehicle detection, desired speed computation, and actuation command generation."
    DTDFVocab:provides LeadVehicleDetectionInsight, DesiredSpeedComputationInsight, ActuationCommandAction
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Safety-critical control and configuration of ACC from a remote location, including enabling/disabling ACC functionality and setting desired speed or emergency commands."
    DTDFVocab:provides ACCControlInsight, EmergencyBrakingAction, DynamicSpeedOverrideAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance VehicleStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization of vehicle state data (speed, acceleration, sensor inputs) for situational awareness by operators."
]

instance DrivingPathInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Display of projected driving path based on object tracking and vehicle state data for path planning validation."
]

instance ObjectTrackInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visualization of detected object tracks (lead vehicle, obstacles) for collision avoidance and ACC system monitoring."
]

instance ReportGenerationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated generation of reports for post-analysis of vehicle state data and ACC system performance."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance LeadVehicleDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detection of lead vehicles via sensor data processing for ACC system computations."
]

instance DesiredSpeedComputationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Computation of desired speed based on dynamic distance thresholds (e.g., time-to-collision metrics) for ACC control."
]

instance ActuationCommandAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Generation of actuation commands (speed adjustments, braking) sent back to the vehicle for execution by on-board actuators."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ACCControlInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Remote operational control of ACC functionality including enabling/disabling ACC and setting desired speed."
]

instance EmergencyBrakingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Execution of emergency braking commands to prevent collisions or unsafe conditions."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance DynamicSpeedOverrideAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remote override of desired speed for ACC system adjustments or emergency scenarios."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **Virtual-to-Physical Interaction** in this DTS is governed by control flows originating from the **Digital Twin Entity** and targeting the **Physical Twin Entity** (vehicle actuators). Key aspects include:
  1. **Command Types**:
     - **Motor Actuation Commands**: Generated by the **Actuation FE** to adjust engine torque or propulsion systems.
     - **Steering Servo Commands**: Issued by the **Actuation FE** to control steering angle or directional adjustments.
     - **ACC Control Commands**: Produced by the **Controlling FE** (Adaptive Cruise Control logic) to regulate throttle/braking or maintain safe following distances.
  2. **Control Message Formats**:
     - Standardized via the **Data Translation FE**, which ensures message serialization, unit alignment (e.g., degrees for steering, N·m for torque), and compatibility between SW components.
     - No explicit protocol (e.g., CAN bus, ROS2 topics) is detailed for physical actuation commands, but the **Actuation FE** implies direct integration with vehicle control systems.
  3. **Validation & Authentication Mechanisms**:
     - **Implicit Validation**: The **Data Translation FE** aligns message types and units, but no explicit validation (e.g., checksums, digital signatures) is documented for command integrity.
     - **No Authentication**: No cryptographic or access-control mechanisms are mentioned for securing control commands.
  4. **Feedback Systems**:
     - **State Feedback Loop**: Physical actuator states (e.g., motor torque, servo angles) are collected by the **Data Collecting FE** and relayed back to the **Digital Twin Entity** for real-time validation.
     - **Conflict Resolution**: Not explicitly detailed, but the **Controlling FE** (ACC logic) likely prioritizes safety-critical commands (e.g., emergency braking) over non-critical adjustments.
  5. **Command Execution Confirmation**:
     - Assumed via the **Actuation FE**, which receives feedback from physical actuators (e.g., confirmed torque application or steering angle achievement). No explicit acknowledgment protocol is documented.
  6. **Control Flow Path**:
     - **Digital Twin → Physical Twin**: **Controlling FE** (ACC logic) → **Actuation FE** → Vehicle actuators (motor/servo/steering).
     - **Feedback Loop**: Actuator states → **Data Collecting FE** → **Digital Twin Entity** (for closed-loop control)."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin System (DTS) enforces a **100 ms clock synchronization accuracy** between physical and digital components to enable computation offloading and coordination, adhering to hardware limitations. This temporal granularity ensures deterministic behavior for safety-critical vehicular applications, where stricter synchronization (e.g., sub-100 ms) is mandated by standards but not achievable due to hardware constraints. The system does not explicitly specify varying time scales for different services or latency requirements beyond this synchronization threshold, but the 100 ms constraint implies a **real-time operational envelope** for time-sensitive functions (e.g., sensor fusion, actuation, or device coordination)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DTS orchestrates its components using a **ROS2-based architecture**, where each **Functional Entity (FE)** is implemented as an **individual ROS2 package** containing one or more **ROS2 nodes**. The system maps software components onto **Functional Entities (FEs)** and deploys them across hardware, adhering to the **ISO 23247 RA** while introducing deviations such as the **Physical Twin Entity** (hosting general automotive functionality like *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking*). Coordination is achieved through **ROS2 inter-node communication**, with strict **100 ms clock synchronization** ensuring temporal alignment. The architecture avoids unnecessary FEs from ISO 23247 and splits the *User Interface FE* into **visualization** and **control panel** subcomponents. The **Data Storage** component lacks a direct FE alignment, indicating a gap in the standard’s coverage. No explicit resource-aware scheduling or load-balancing mechanisms are described."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The ISO 23247 standard does not explicitly outline **model fidelity requirements**, **validation/verification methods**, or **uncertainty quantification techniques**. However, the **entity-based architecture** implies that **fidelity** is managed through the **Digital Twin Entity’s (DTE) alignment with the Observable Manufacturing Element (OME)**, where discrepancies may arise from **data inaccuracies, model simplifications, or control delays**. **Error detection and correction mechanisms** are not specified, but the **Data Collection and Device Control Entity** suggests a feedback loop for **real-time adjustments**. **Quality assurance processes** are not detailed, though the standard’s emphasis on **interoperability and modularity** (via Functional Entities) may indirectly support traceability and consistency checks. The absence of explicit guidelines leaves fidelity and validity as **implementation-dependent**, relying on domain-specific practices."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **technical network infrastructure** for this Digital Twin System (DTS) is defined as follows:

1. **Communication Protocols**:
   - **Intra-Entity (Software Components)**: **ROS2 (Robot Operating System 2)** network for modular, topic-based communication between software components (e.g., **Data Collecting FE**, **Presentation FE**, **Controlling FE**).
   - **Inter-Entity (Physical ↔ Digital Twin)**: **Wi-Fi with UDP** at the transport layer for low-latency, high-throughput data exchange between sensors/actuators (Physical Twin) and software components (Digital Twin).

2. **Network Topology and Architecture**:
   - **Hierarchical Model**:
     - **Physical Twin Entity**: Sensors (LiDAR, motor, steering servo) and actuators.
     - **Digital Twin Entity**: Software components (e.g., **Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking**, **Data Translation FE**, **Controlling FE**) deployed on a ROS2-based system.
     - **User Entity**: **3D Visualization** and **Control Panel** interfaces accessed via the **User Interface FE**.
   - **Data Propagation**: As shown in Fig. 2, sensor data flows from the Physical Twin → Digital Twin → User Entity, with control signals flowing in reverse.

3. **Bandwidth and Latency Requirements**:
   - **LiDAR Data**: High bandwidth due to raw point cloud volume; preprocessing (clustering/tracking) reduces payload before visualization.
   - **Real-Time Constraints**: **3D Visualization** and **ACC Controlling FE** require **low-latency updates** (likely <100ms end-to-end for critical paths like object tracking).
   - **UDP-Based Communication**: Prioritizes speed over reliability for time-sensitive data (e.g., LiDAR streams, actuation commands).

4. **Reliability and Redundancy Mechanisms**:
   - **Not explicitly detailed**, but implied through:
     - **Data Translation FE**: Ensures message consistency and error handling (e.g., unit conversion validation).
     - **ROS2**: Supports **reliable publishers/subscribers** for critical data (e.g., actuation commands).
   - **No explicit redundancy** (e.g., failover nodes or backup networks) is mentioned.

5. **Security Protocols**:
   - **Not in Document**: No details on encryption (e.g., TLS), access control, or intrusion detection for network communication.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **dt_hosting_deployment** infrastructure for this DTS is characterized by a **modular, on-vehicle-centric design** with the following technical specifics:
  1. **Hosting Platforms**:
     - **Edge Deployment**:
       - **Physical Twin Entity**: Hosts **LiDAR**, **motor**, **steering servo**, and **Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking** components on-vehicle (likely embedded hardware).
       - **Digital Twin Entity**: Runs **Data Collecting FE**, **Presentation FE**, and **3D Visualization** on-vehicle (e.g., NVIDIA Jetpack-enabled platform for ROS2).
     - **No Cloud Deployment**: All processing is localized to the vehicle; no mention of cloud offloading or hybrid models.
  2. **Computational Requirements**:
     - **Point Cloud Processing**: Demands high-performance compute for **clustering and tracking** (e.g., GPU acceleration via NVIDIA Jetpack).
     - **Real-Time Constraints**: Components like **Controlling FE (ACC)** and **Actuation FE** require low-latency execution (≤100 ms).
  3. **Storage Requirements**:
     - **Data Storage FE**: Persists raw/processed data for reporting; no capacity or retention policies specified.
     - **No Distributed Storage**: Storage appears localized to the Digital Twin Entity.
  4. **Scalability Mechanisms**:
     - **Modular Design (R1)**: Components are decoupled (e.g., *Data Pre-Processing FE*, *Actuation FE*), allowing **vertical scaling** (e.g., upgrading embedded hardware) but **no horizontal scaling** (e.g., load balancing) is implied.
  5. **Deployment Models**:
     - **Single-Instance**: Each vehicle hosts its own DTS instance; no multi-vehicle or fleet-wide deployment described.
     - **ROS2-Based**: Components are deployed as ROS2 nodes, with dependencies managed via package manifests.
  6. **Availability/Reliability Assurance**:
     - **No Redundancy**: Single-point failures (e.g., Wi-Fi dropout, sensor malfunction) are not mitigated by backup systems.
     - **Real-Time OS**: **Not Explicitly Excluded**: While NVIDIA Jetpack and ROS2 are mentioned, the document does not confirm or deny the use of a **Real-Time Operating System (RTOS)** for critical components.
  7. **Hardware Constraints**:
     - **On-Vehicle Constraints**: Limited by embedded hardware (e.g., power, thermal) but no specific models or specs provided."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system aligns with the **ISO 23247 – Digital Twin Framework for Manufacturing** standard, specifically **Part 2 (Reference Architecture)**, which defines an entity-based framework without mandating specific technologies. While the standard references **OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA)** as an example for implementing **Functional Entities (FEs)** (e.g., for data collection or control), it does not enforce compliance with OPC UA or other protocols like **IEC 62541**. Similarly, **ISO/IEC 30173** (a separate standard for digital twin interoperability) is not cited as a requirement of ISO 23247. The architecture emphasizes **interoperability** through modular entities and functional decomposition but leaves implementation details (e.g., communication protocols, data formats) to user discretion, provided they adhere to the standard’s entity-based structure."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system must adhere to **ISO 15622:2018** for **Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) performance requirements**, ensuring compliance with predefined safety and operational constraints. The system's **real-time data transmission** (e.g., V2X services) is governed by **3GPP TS 22.185 v15.0.0**, which specifies a **maximum network latency of 100 ms** for critical data exchange. Additionally, the system must account for **limited on-vehicle storage** due to the high volume of real-time data, necessitating efficient data handling to prevent local corruption or tampering risks. The **model-driven architecture** for interactive Digital Twin cockpits (as referenced in [37]) implies a structured approach to system integrity, though explicit cybersecurity protocols (e.g., authentication, access control) or threat mitigation strategies are not detailed in the provided documents."
]
